reaction.name: Formate transport in via proton symport

body.p: Notes: fatty acid synthesis is not well characterized in LGG, so this reaction relies purely on use of annotation
Notes: Known heterofermenter. Data from lactocaseibacillus casei supports directionality/reversibility of this reaction, along with observations of problems with acidification of LABs.

Notes: The malic enzyme and the malolactic enzyme are structurally similar, suggesting that the GPR annotation is correct. Additionally, it is well known that lactobacilli survive in dairy using these enzymes as mechanism (PMID: 37681961)

reaction.name: Butyryl acyl carrier protein malonyl CoA C acyltransferase

body.p: Notes: fatty acid synthesis is not well characterized in LGG, so this reaction relies purely on use of annotation

Notes: There is some potential role for beatine in LABs, but the pathways are not fully understood. There is evidence that it is taken up from the environment in many papers involving supplementation, but nothing more that associatation

reaction.name: ATP + THF-polyglutamate(n) + L-Glutamate <=> ADP + Orthophosphate + THF-polyglutamate(n+1)
Dihydrofolate:NADP+ oxidoreductase One carbon pool by folate / Folate biosynthesis EC:1.5.1.3
ATP:pantothenate 4-phosphotransferase Pantothenate and CoA biosynthesis EC:2.7.1.33
Tetradecanoyl-[acp] + H2O => Acyl-carrier protein + Tetradecanoic acid

reaction.name: ADP + (6S)-6beta-Hydroxy-1,4,5,6-tetrahydronicotinamide-adenine dinucleotide phosphate => AMP + Orthophosphate + NADPH
L-Threonine + ATP + HCO3- + H+ => L-Threonylcarbamoyladenylate + Diphosphate + H2O
Acyl-[acyl-carrier protein] + Orthophosphate <=> Acyl phosphate + Acyl-carrier protein

reaction.name: Ribose transport in via proton symporter

body.p: Notes: suggested to not be utilized, but the gene is reasonable PMCID: PMC2746127

reaction.name: D sorbitol transport in via proton symport

body.p: Notes: another LGG strain can do this https://doi.org/10.1016/j.lwt.2024.116443
Notes: trehelose has been used as a preservative in LGG specifically https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jfoodeng.2024.112252

reaction.name: Tetradecanoyl acyl carrier protein malonyl CoA C acyltransferase

body.p: Notes: fatty acid synthesis is not well characterized in LGG, so this reaction relies purely on use of annotation
Notes: occum's razor for shikimate and trp metabolism
Notes: occum's razor for riboflavin. DO data suggests effect on growth when riboflavin removed
Notes: occum's razor for B12 synthesis
Notes: effect on growth in DMs when removed
Notes: occum's razor for riboflavin. DO data suggests effect on growth when riboflavin removed
Notes: occum's razor for riboflavin. DO data suggests effect on growth when riboflavin removed
Notes: occum's razor for riboflavin. DO data suggests effect on growth when riboflavin removed
Notes: occum's razor for proline. Effect in DO data when removed
Notes: occum's razor appraoch to valine leucine synthesis canonical pathway from KEGG
Notes: occum's razor for folate. DO data suggests effect when removed from media for folate
Notes: occum's razor for arginine. Effect of arg when removed from media
Notes: occum's razor for pantothenate. Effect when pantothenate removed from media
Notes: reaction in related lactocaseibacillus pabuli and occum's razor for tyrosine/trp
Notes: fructose as an alternative LGG sugar substrate

reaction.name: Adenosyl cobyrinate a,c diamide + 4 L-Glutamine + 4 ATP + 4 H2O <=> Adenosyl cobyrinate hexaamide + 4 L-Glutamate + 4 Orthophosphate + 4 ADP

body.p: Notes: occum's razor for B12 synthesis
Notes: occum's razor for pyroxidal. DO reveals effect on growth when removed
Notes: occum's razor for folate. DO data suggests effect when removed
Notes: occum's razor for biotin, DO data suggests effect on growth when biotin removed
Notes: occum's razor for folate. DO data suggests effect when removed
Notes: occum's razor for riboflavin. DO data suggests effect on growth when riboflavin removed
Notes: Effect on growth when removed for many forms of DMs
Notes: Effect on growth when removed for many forms of DMs

Notes: Directionality from BIGG database, cofactor was provided in the BIGG database. Localization from psort: cytosolic. Provided reference showed the candidate gene is AldA and AldB in E. coli and Pseudomonas are responsible for this reaction. Blasted the E. coli against L. rham and got this as the top hit of 35% similiarity.
Notes: Effect on growth when removed for many forms of DMs. No identified papers regarding use of extracellular component
Notes: observed growth aerobically in DMs

reaction.name: Pantothenate reversible transport via proton symport

body.p: Notes: Effect on growth when removed for many forms of DMs. No identified papers regarding use of or mechanism of transfer for extracellular component
Notes: effect on growth in DMs when removed

reaction.name: cytosine transport in via proton symport

body.p: Notes: effect on growth in DMs when removed
Notes: Effect on growth when removed for many forms of DMs. No identified papers regarding use of extracellular component
Notes: known to be taken up PMID: 37752249
Notes: Effect on growth when removed for many forms of DMs. Support for PTS sugar transporters https://doi.org/10.1038/s41522-023-00431-2

reaction.name: Dipeptide transport via ABC system gly asn

body.p: Notes: effect on growth in DMs when removed
Notes: effect on growth in DMs when removed

reaction.name: Glycine Reversible Transport via Proton Symport

body.p: Notes: Effect on growth when removed for many forms of DMs. No identified papers regarding use of or mechanism of transfer for extracellular component

reaction.name: L-isoleucine reversible transport via proton symport

body.p: Notes: LGG amino acid auxotrophy in vitro from DM. Degradation significantly increased in mice PMCID: PMC8541209

reaction.name: L-leucine reversible transport via proton symport

body.p: Notes: Effect on growth when removed for many forms of DMs. In mouse and in vitro experiments, guanine was reduced ps://doi.org/10.1038/s41522-024-00486-9

reaction.name: L-lysine reversible transport via proton symport

body.p: Notes: Effect on growth when removed for many forms of DMs. No identified papers regarding use of or mechanism of transfer for extracellular component

reaction.name: manganese transport in via proton symport

body.p: Notes: Effect on growth when removed for many forms of DMs. Many versions of MRS also contain Magnesium

reaction.name: 2-oxoglutarate + coenzyme A + NAD+ → succinyl-CoA + CO2 + NADH

reaction.name: 1 alpha D Galactosyl myo inositol galactohydrolase

body.p: Notes: gene identified during dm57 curation, occum's razor for myo-inositol
Notes: gene identified during dm57 curation
Notes: evidence for other carbon sugar sources across various literature
Notes: gene identified during dm57 curation
Notes: gene identified during dm57 curation
Notes: gene identified during dm57 curation
Notes: gene identified during unblocking of nucleotide synthesis
Notes: edited version of an AGORA reaction
Notes: edited version of an AGORA reaction
Notes: edited version of an AGORA reaction
Notes: edited version of an AGORA reaction
Notes: edited version of an AGORA reaction
Notes: edited version of an AGORA reaction
Notes: occum's razor appraoch to canonical pathway from KEGG
Notes: edited version of an AGORA reaction, used in the occum's razor approach to shikimate pathway from dropout data
Notes: added in order to process coomplex sugars properly, this was a fixed version of an AGORA based reaction
Notes: added in order to process coomplex sugars properly, this was a fixed version of an AGORA based reaction
Notes: added in order to process coomplex sugars properly, this was a fixed version of an AGORA based reaction
Notes: occum's razor appraoch to canonical pathway from KEGG
Notes: occum's razor from DM57 curation
Notes: ADP ribose is only known to be added as a post-translational modification in bacteria.

Notes: Directionality from BIGG database. Localization from psort: cytosolic membrane. This enzyme is a pyruvate oxidase, which is what Kegg said it would be. Cross referenced to Biocyc and this protein came up. In a bidirectional blast against our related organisms, this blasted to pyruvate oxidases in all of them.
Notes: LGG grows well in leucine DO media

Notes: directionality determined from the kegg database, Rxn Id came from metacyc biocyc ID, cofactor was found in metacyc for this reaction. Localization from psort: unknown. This candidate was found once it was discovered that indole-3-acetate goes through a sponaneous change to indole-3-lactate provided reference. This protein is in Clostridium and when blasted against lacto, got a 35% similarity to the candidate.

reaction.name: Adenine transport in via proton symport

body.p: Notes: Effect on growth when removed for many forms of DMs. No identified papers regarding use of extracellular component
Notes: effect on growth in DMs when removed
Notes: Effect on growth when removed for many forms of DMs. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41522-023-00431-2 suggests citrate symporter
Notes: papers suggest increase in carnitine in mice, but not for the directionality in MERLIN PMCID: PMC11700500
Notes: effect on growth in DMs when removed
Notes: suggested effect in goose model PMCID: PMC10954633
Notes: suggested effect in goose model PMCID: PMC10954633
Notes: suggested effect in goose model PMCID: PMC10954633
Notes: suggested effect in goose model PMCID: PMC10954633

reaction.name: 1.0 H2O (in) + 1.0 ATP (in) + 1.0 L-Threonine (out) => 1.0 L-Threonine (in) + 1.0 ADP (in) + 1.0 Orthophosphate (in)

body.p: Notes: Effect on growth when removed for many forms of DMs

Notes: Added reaction from kegg, because same gene as this reaction rules the new reaction per KEGG comment This enzyme in the enteric bacteria also possesses chorismate mutase EC 5.4.99.5 activity, and converts chorismate into prephenate.

reaction.name: L-Methionine (S)-S-oxide + Thioredoxin => L-Methionine + Thioredoxin disulfide + H2O

body.p: Notes: biochemistry says that met readily oxidizes to L-met-SO, and the genes are well known and annotated in LGG. Adding reaction met + O would yield met-S-oxide https://doi.org/10.1016/j.freeradbiomed.2008.03.005
Notes: known to be taken up PMID: 31103290

reaction.name: L tryptophan reversible transport via proton symport

body.p: Notes: known to be taken up PMID: 31103290
Notes: occum's razor for pantothenate. Effect when pantothenate removed from media
Notes: occum's razor for pantothenate. Effect when pantothenate removed from media
Notes: occum's razor for riboflavin. DO data suggests effect on growth when riboflavin removed
Notes: occum's razor for biotin, DO data suggests effect on growth when biotin removed
Notes: occum's razor for tyrosine and trp
Notes: occum's razor for valine and leucine
Notes: implied additional sugar carbon source

fbc:geneProduct.fbc:name: Multidrug ABC transporter ATP-binding and permease components
Multidrug ABC transporter ATP-binding and permease components
PTS system cellobiose-specific transporter subunit IIA
Cytochrome D ubiquinol oxidase subunit I
Cytochrome D ubiquinol oxidase subunit II
Probable tRNA sulfurtransferase (EC 2.8.1.4) (Sulfur carrier protein ThiS sulfurtransferase) (Thiamine biosynthesis protein ThiI) (tRNA 4-thiouridine synthase)
Adenylate kinase (AK) (EC 2.7.4.3) (ATP-AMP transphosphorylase) (ATP:AMP phosphotransferase) (Adenylate monophosphate kinase)
dTTP/UTP pyrophosphatase (dTTPase/UTPase) (EC 3.6.1.9) (Nucleoside triphosphate pyrophosphatase) (Nucleotide pyrophosphatase) (Nucleotide PPase)
Acetyl-coenzyme A carboxylase carboxyl transferase subunit beta (ACCase subunit beta) (Acetyl-CoA carboxylase carboxyltransferase subunit beta) (EC 2.1.3.15)
Biotin carboxyl carrier protein of acetyl-CoA carboxylase
Corrinoid adenosyltransferase (EC 2.5.1.17) (Cob(II)alamin adenosyltransferase) (Cob(II)yrinic acid a,c-diamide adenosyltransferase) (Cobinamide/cobalamin adenosyltransferase)
Diaminopimelate epimerase (DAP epimerase) (EC 5.1.1.7) (PLP-independent amino acid racemase)
Riboflavin biosynthesis protein [Includes: Riboflavin kinase (EC 2.7.1.26) (Flavokinase); FMN adenylyltransferase (EC 2.7.7.2) (FAD pyrophosphorylase) (FAD synthase)]
Fumarate hydratase class II (Fumarase C) (EC 4.2.1.2) (Aerobic fumarase) (Iron-independent fumarase)

Bifunctional protein GlmU [Includes: UDP-N-acetylglucosamine pyrophosphorylase (EC 2.7.7.23) (N-acetylglucosamine-1-phosphate uridyltransferase); Glucosamine-1-phosphate N-acetyltransferase (EC 2.3.1.157)]

Glutamine--fructose-6-phosphate aminotransferase [isomerizing] (EC 2.6.1.16) (D-fructose-6-phosphate amidotransferase) (GFAT) (Glucosamine-6-phosphate synthase) (Hexosephosphate aminotransferase) (L-glutamine--D-fructose-6-phosphate amidotransferase)

Multifunctional fusion protein [Includes: dITP/XTP pyrophosphatase (EC 3.6.1.66) (Non-canonical purine NTP pyrophosphatase) (Non-standard purine NTP pyrophosphatase) (Nucleoside-triphosphate diphosphatase) (Nucleoside-triphosphate pyrophosphatase) (NTPase); Glutamate racemase (EC 5.1.1.3)]

Isopentenyl-diphosphate delta-isomerase (IPP isomerase) (EC 5.3.3.2) (Isopentenyl diphosphate:dimethylallyl diphosphate isomerase) (Isopentenyl pyrophosphate isomerase) (Type 2 isopentenyl diphosphate isomerase) (IDI-2)
5-methyltetrahydropteroyltriglutamate--homocysteine methyltransferase (EC 2.1.1.14) (Cobalamin-independent methionine synthase) (Methionine synthase, vitamin-B12 independent isozyme)
Malonate-semialdehyde dehydrogenase (MSA dehydrogenase) (EC 1.2.1.27) (Methylmalonate semialdehyde dehydrogenase) (MMSA dehydrogenase) (MSDH)

Coenzyme A biosynthesis bifunctional protein CoaBC (DNA/pantothenate metabolism flavoprotein) (Phosphopantothenoylcysteine synthetase/decarboxylase) (PPCS-PPCDC) [Includes: Phosphopantothenoylcysteine decarboxylase (PPC decarboxylase) (PPC-DC) (EC 4.1.1.36) (CoaC); Phosphopantothenate--cysteine ligase (EC 6.3.2.5) (CoaB) (Phosphopantothenoylcysteine synthetase) (PPC synthetase) (PPC-S)]

Phosphoribosylformylglycinamidine synthase subunit PurL (FGAM synthase) (EC 6.3.5.3) (Formylglycinamide ribonucleotide amidotransferase subunit II) (FGAR amidotransferase II) (FGAR-AT II) (Glutamine amidotransferase PurL) (Phosphoribosylformylglycinamidine synthase subunit II)

Phosphoribosylformylglycinamidine synthase subunit PurS (FGAM synthase) (EC 6.3.5.3) (Formylglycinamide ribonucleotide amidotransferase subunit III) (FGAR amidotransferase III) (FGAR-AT III) (Phosphoribosylformylglycinamidine synthase subunit III)
N5-carboxyaminoimidazole ribonucleotide synthase (N5-CAIR synthase) (EC 6.3.4.18) (5-(carboxyamino)imidazole ribonucleotide synthetase)
Dihydrolipoamide acetyltransferase component of pyruvate dehydrogenase complex (EC 2.3.1.-)
N5-carboxyaminoimidazole ribonucleotide mutase (N5-CAIR mutase) (EC 5.4.99.18) (5-(carboxyamino)imidazole ribonucleotide mutase)
Amino acid ABC transporter permease component
Amino acid ABC transporter permease and substrate binding components
Branched-chain amino acid ABC transporter ATP-binding component
Branched-chain amino acid ABC transporter substrate binding component
Branched-chain amino acid ABC transporter ATP-binding component
Branched-chain amino acid ABC transporter permease component
Amino acid ABC transporter ATP-binding protein
Branched-chain amino acid ABC transporter permease component
Amino acid ABC transporter permease component
Amino acid ABC transporter ATP-binding protein
Amino acid ABC transporter permease component
Amino acid ABC transporter substrate-binding protein
Glycine betaine/carnitine/choline ABC transporter ATP-binding component
Glycine betaine/carnitine/choline ABC transporter permease component
Glycine betaine/carnitine/choline ABC transporter substrate binding component
Cytochrome D ubiquinol oxidase subunit I
Cytochrome D ubiquinol oxidase subunit II
Cytochrome D ABC transporter ATP-binding and permease components
Cytochrome D ABC transporter ATP-binding and permease components

Glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase (Acyl-PO4 G3P acyltransferase) (Acyl-phosphate--glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase) (G3P acyltransferase) (GPAT) (EC 2.3.1.275) (Lysophosphatidic acid synthase) (LPA synthase)
Gamma-glutamyl phosphate reductase (GPR) (EC 1.2.1.41) (Glutamate-5-semialdehyde dehydrogenase) (Glutamyl-gamma-semialdehyde dehydrogenase) (GSA dehydrogenase)

Beta-ketoacyl-[acyl-carrier-protein] synthase III (Beta-ketoacyl-ACP synthase III) (KAS III) (EC 2.3.1.180) (3-oxoacyl-[acyl-carrier-protein] synthase 3) (3-oxoacyl-[acyl-carrier-protein] synthase III)

Probable nicotinate-nucleotide adenylyltransferase (EC 2.7.7.18) (Deamido-NAD(+) diphosphorylase) (Deamido-NAD(+) pyrophosphorylase) (Nicotinate mononucleotide adenylyltransferase) (NaMN adenylyltransferase)
Spermidine/putrescine ABC transporter substrate binding component
Small ribosomal subunit biogenesis GTPase RsgA (EC 3.6.1.-)
Metal ion ABC transporter substrate binding component
Bifunctional protein FolD [Includes: Methylenetetrahydrofolate dehydrogenase (EC 1.5.1.5); Methenyltetrahydrofolate cyclohydrolase (EC 3.5.4.9)]
Metal ion ABC transporter substrate binding component

groups:group.groups:name: Tyrosine, Tryptophan, and Amino Acid Metabolism
